clues.clue: Despite its name, this common first-trimester symptom can strike any time of day.
Practice contractions that come and go irregularly throughout pregnancy.
The Hollywood-classic moment when the amniotic sac ruptures before labor.
Late-pregnancy urge to deep-clean, organize, and prepare the home for baby.
Protective seal of the cervix that's expelled as labor approaches.
Tightly wrapping a baby in a blanket to mimic the snugness of the womb.
Skin-to-skin contact method popularized for premature babies.
AAP-recommended sleep position to reduce SIDS risk.
The sticky, dark green-black first stool a newborn passes.
Patches of dry, scaly, harmless skin commonly found on a newborn's scalp.
Average weight in whole pounds of a full-term newborn.
Approximate hours per day a newborn spends sleeping.
Soft spots on a baby's head where the skull bones haven't fused.
Approximate number of bones a newborn is born with — more than an adult.
Startle reflex in which a newborn flings out their arms in response to a loud noise or sudden movement.
A baby cow.
A baby kangaroo.
A baby goat.
A baby swan.
A baby platypus or echidna.
Pacifier-sucking baby in a yellow jumpsuit and red bow, member of the Simpson family.
Football-headed, world-domination-plotting baby on Family Guy.
Briefcase-toting executive in the 2017 DreamWorks animated film.
Pixar baby in The Incredibles whose multiple powers (lasers, shapeshifting) emerge late in the film.
Half-vampire daughter of Bella and Edward in The Twilight Saga: Breaking Dawn.
Typical age in months when most babies first roll over.
Common two-syllable word that's often a baby's first.
Average age in months when babies start sitting up unassisted.
Average age in months for first independent steps.
Cognitive milestone, developed around 8 months, of understanding that objects still exist when out of sight.

finalJeopardy.clue: Born July 25, 1978 in Oldham, England, she became the world's first 'test tube' baby — conceived via in vitro fertilization.
